exemplar.title: Should Lippert change and expand their online lesson service to attract more customers?

detailedExemplarData.title: Should Lippert change and expand their online lesson service to attract more customers?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Accurately identifies âchangeâ as the key concept and maintains conceptual focus throughout multiple sections
Purposeful use of change lens in Force-Field analysis and COVID-19 discussion
Consistent references back to Lippertâs context (teacher base, pricing, student numbers)
Reflective comparison of traditional vs. innovative positioning integrates concept in conclusion
weaknesses: Integration in Ansoff and Marketing-mix sections sometimes drifts into generic growth commentary
Some global demand claims are not explicitly tied back to the change concept
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although a few tool discussions occasionally veer into generic commentary, the key concept of change is consistently and thoughtfully woven through framing, analysis, and conclusion. The breadth of application and conceptual insight justify awarding full marks.

strengths: Uses three distinct, relevant documents (interview, website/Instagram, market-cost article) within the 3â5 range
Primary interview adds authenticity and firsthand perspective
Market-cost article provides concrete price comparisons
weaknesses: Coverage is somewhat narrow and lacks triangulation beyond the three sources
Inconsistent shorthand citations (SD1) and missing full bibliographic details
Depth could be enhanced by critiquing source reliability and adding more market data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides a sufficient range of relevant documents and demonstrates moderate depth, but limited triangulation and citation inconsistencies prevent a top-band award.

strengths: Appropriate selection of three recognized business tools (Force-Field, Ansoff matrix, Marketing mix)
Force-Field tool is well explained and correctly applied
Marketing-mix structure (incorporating People) aids clarity
Clear introduction of Ansoff classification for Lippertâs expansion
weaknesses: Tool applications often remain descriptive without deeper justification
Some assertions (e.g., âminimal riskâ) lack Lippert-specific evidence
Cost estimates for international staffing and deeper risk evaluation are missing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The internal analysis employs an adequate range of tools with generally relevant application, but uneven critical depth and insufficient justification of some claims limit the performance to the middle band.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Data selection (pricing, student/teacher numbers, CAGR) is sufficient for the analysis
Force-Field weighing demonstrates clear evaluation of driving vs. restraining forces
Multi-tool analytical approach (Force-Field, Ansoff, Marketing mix) shows sustained integration
Use of multiple secondary sources (SD3, SD4) enriches discussion of barriers
weaknesses: Diagram lacks axis labels, title, and legend for clarity
Restraining force scores are unquantified and require justification
Competitor price examples and profit-margin analysis are missing
Depth of evaluation varies between sections
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis moves well beyond description with mostly effective evaluation and integration across tools. Minor gaps in labeling, quantitative justification, and comparative data prevent the topmost band but warrant a high mark.

strengths: Conclusion synthesizes key findings from tools and relates back to the research question
Evidence from earlier analysis is referenced to support recommendations
weaknesses: Stops short of a definitive yes/no recommendation, offering only a conditional âconsider expansionâ
Does not reconcile earlier voiced uncertainties (e.g., cost implications) or provide fresh evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion partially answers the research question and aligns with the evidence, but the lack of a decisive recommendation and unresolved uncertainties limit it to the middle band.

strengths: Logical organization with clear introduction, tool-based sections, and conclusion
Explicit research question and well-formatted section headings guide the reader
weaknesses: Table of contents formatting is inconsistent and nested entries misaligned
Introduction ends mid-sentence and some notes (e.g., technology costs) appear disconnected
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall structure is appropriate and coherent, with only minor formatting and completeness issues, thus meriting the full mark.

strengths: All required IA elements are present (title page, word count, headings, bibliography)
Visuals are labelled and sources cited, supporting clarity
weaknesses: Citation formatting is inconsistent and incomplete across sources
A stray LaTeX artifact and misaligned table formatting detract slightly from professionalism
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets all presentation requirements with clear visual aids and complete elements; minor inconsistencies in formatting prevent any deduction beyond those isolated issues.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure is evident (TOC, introduction, tool-based sections, conclusion).  Minor formatting issues (empty columns, inconsistent headings) detract slightly but overall organisation remains appropriate.

All cited materials are directly relevant to the research question, and the primary interview adds authenticity.  Depth is moderateâstatistics such as CAGR and price comparisons are includedâbut coverage is narrow and lacks triangulation, so the evidence is sufficient rather than wide-ranging.
Required IA elementsâtitle, word count, section headings, bibliographyâare present.  Visuals are labelled and sources cited, fulfilling the presentation expectations.
Three distinct documents are cited (interview, Lippert website/Instagram, market-cost article), fitting the required 3-5 range.  No excess or shortage of sources is apparent.

Each tool is applied to Lippert with reasonable relevance: the Force-Field is fully populated; Ansoff classifies the move as market penetration; 4P/7P elements are linked to pricing and promotion realities.  Yet the applications often stop at description, and some assertions (e.g., âminimal riskâ) are not substantiated, so the effectiveness is mixed.

Data include lesson prices, number of teachers/students, and a CAGR figure.  These are used within the Force-Field and Marketing-mix discussion, showing adequateânot extensiveâselection and utilisation.  Some claims (e.g., international demand) lack supporting data, limiting the strand to âadequateâ.

Analysis moves beyond description: the Force-Field weighs forces, the Ansoff matrix judges risk, and the marketing-mix comments on pricing strategy.  Nevertheless, evaluation depth is uneven and integration across sections is limited, so the work fits âsome analysisâ better than âmostly effectiveâ.

The student stops short of a definite answer, recommending that Lippert âconsiderâ expansion rather than stating yes or no.  This addresses the RQ only partially, meeting the descriptor for a partial answer.

The IA employs three recognised BM tools (Force-Field, Ansoff, elements of the 7P mix).  This is an adequate, balanced selection for the decision type, though not especially extensive or novel, hence âadequateâ rather than âeffectiveâ.

The conclusion synthesises findings and links back to the analysis, so there is some consistency with earlier evidence.  However, several points are asserted without fresh support and uncertainties voiced earlier (e.g., cost implications) are not reconciled, so alignment is only partial.

Lippertâs circumstances, resources and constraints are referenced throughout (e.g., current teacher base in Canada, 50 existing online students, pricing versus local competitors).  These details are analysed rather than merely described, and each major tool is applied to the organisationâs real context.  Nonetheless, some statements remain generic (e.g., blanket claims about âglobal demandâ without Lippert-specific data), preventing fully integrated analysis across every paragraph.

The student demonstrates a solid grasp of the key concept of change, defining it clearly and repeatedly linking it to the decision about expanding online lessons.  References to COVID-19 as a catalyst, the Force-Field discussion of driving and restraining forces, and the closing reflection on Lippertâs âtraditionalâ versus âinnovativeâ positioning all show purposeful use of the concept.  However, the integration is unevenâsections such as the Ansoff and Marketing-mix discussion sometimes drift into generic growth commentary without explicitly threading back to changeâso full, seamless integration is not yet evident.

annotations.comment: The title page clearly states the IA level and focus, fulfilling essential presentation requirements and establishing a professional start.
Identifying âChangeâ as the key concept is accurate and sets a clear conceptual lens for the entire assessment.
The research question is explicitly stated and focused, guiding the analysis effectively within the scope of the IA.
The introduction header is clear and follows IA conventions, aiding navigability.
The diagram lacks axis labels and a title, making it hard to interpret force categories; label axes and include a legend.
Restraining force scores are listed but not justified; explain quantitatively why each factor was rated (e.g., criteria for a 3).
Linking to SD2 enhances credibility, showing data driven analysis of teacher availability challenges.
Use of multiple secondary sources (SD3, SD4) adds depth to discussion of technological and training barriers.
CAGR data is cited but not critiqued; assess source reliability and consider triangulating with additional market data.
The summary calculates totals but misses analysis of the narrow margin (14 vs. 11); discuss implications of this small difference.
The Ansoff matrix section is introduced clearly, indicating appropriate tool selection.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The matrix table formatting is confusing with misaligned cells; restructure for clarity and readability.
Classification as market penetration is logical, but risk evaluation relies on generic forecasts; integrate Lippert-specific evidence.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Revenue impact of reduced pricing is not evaluated; analyse potential profit margin changes to inform recommendation.
The table of contents includes all main sections but formatting is inconsistent and nested entries are misaligned; refine headings and indentation.
A stray LaTeX artifact (ââ±ðâ) appears without context; remove or replace with the intended content.
Background information is informative, but citations use shorthand (SD1) without full referencing; ensure consistent in-text citation style.
The introduction ends mid-sentence with âThe IA will includeâ; complete this outline or remove for a polished introduction.
Identifying the interview as primary research demonstrates good sourcing of firsthand data for analysis.
The definition of âchangeâ is generic; deepen integration by explicitly linking to Lippertâs decision context throughout.
The Force Field tool is well explained with citation, demonstrating correct selection and application.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The Marketing Mix header and subsections are well structured, aiding reader navigation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Promotion analysis effectively identifies existing channels, demonstrating sound application of the tool.
Pricing discussion lacks competitor price examples; include specific data points to strengthen comparative analysis.
Including People demonstrates comprehensive tool use, but cost estimates for hiring international staff are missing.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Discussion of hiring international teachers outlines challenges but needs quantitative costâbenefit analysis.
Process improvements are suggested generally; specify standardized procedures or platforms for implementation.
An isolated note on technology costs appears disconnected; integrate into broader process or cost analysis.
The conclusion heading is clear, marking a distinct end to analytical sections.
Conclusion synthesizes tools but stops short of a decisive recommendation; explicitly state yes/no to answer the research question.
Bibliography section is present, satisfying presentation requirements for supporting documentation.
Citations are inconsistently formatted and incomplete; use a uniform referencing style and provide full publication details.
